boxes.label: algorithm
boxes.text:
hetic Petri Dish
Input: Set of motifs already evaluated X = . Initia
set of motifs for ground-truth evaluation Xeval. Perfo
mance of motifs P = . Number of motifs to generat
M. Number of motifs to select K s.t. K << M. Num
ber of NAS search iterations L. Additional Petri dis
hyperparameters (Appendix A.2 and A.3).
for l = 1 to L do
Ground-truth Evaluation: Train each motif in Xeva
in the full-scale setting and obtain full-scale validatio
set performance Peval. Enlarge P : P = P ∪ Peva
Enlarge X : X = X ∪ Xeval.
Petri dish Training: Train the Petri dish model usin
X and P (see sub-section 3.1).
Architecture Search: Generate a set of M new motif
(Xnew) using X and P as inputs to the NAS method
Performance Prediction: Run Synthetic Petri Dis
inference for Xnew (sub-section 3.2) to obtain moti
performance predictions.
Selection: Pick top K motifs (denoted by set Xtop) ou
of the set Xnew based on their predicted performanc
Set Xeval = Xtop.
end for
Output: The motif within X with the best ground-tru
ormance
